place: New Hyde Park New York, New York

facts.text: New Hyde Park’s name comes from the 1600s when Clark renamed the Dongan Estate “Hyde Park” after his wife’s English home, Hyde Hall.
In 1837 the Long Island Railroad reached New Hyde Park, but for years no station existed until the town’s growth demanded one.
The Vanderbilt Mansion National Historic Site, once Frederick William Vanderbilt’s spring and autumn home, now preserves the family’s legacy in the village.
Portions of the 1994 film “Wolf” starring Jack Nicholson were filmed at the nearby Vanderbilt Mansion, linking Hollywood to New Hyde Park.
New Hyde Park’s roots trace back to the 1600s, making it one of the oldest settlements on Long Island.
